items.title: License Your Dog in Boston
items.description: Dog owners must license dogs older than six months before the annual City deadline.
items.cost: $15-$30, waived for eligible residents 70+ applying by mail or in person
items.source: City of Boston Animal Care and Control

items.title: Apply for Boston Property Tax Exemptions
items.description: Eligible homeowners can file for the current fiscal year property tax exemption by the annual deadline.

items.title: Apply for the Boston Digital Equity Fund
items.description: Boston-based organizations can apply for digital navigation, training, and device access funding that supports residents' online access to services and opportunities.
items.source: City of Boston Innovation and Technology

items.title: Apply for Boston Small Community Grants
items.description: Nonprofits can apply for small City trust grants supporting direct-impact community work, recreation, beautification, and field trips for Boston residents.

items.title: Apply for an Age Strong Rolling Grant
items.description: Boston nonprofits, civic associations, and senior groups can apply for small-scale funding that supports older adult programming and social connection.
items.source: City of Boston Age Strong Commission
